label: Outcome
description: Provide users with confirmation that they have successfully completed their intended task. Users need to know their action has been received and processed, and what will happen next.
purpose: Close the interaction clearly, confirm what happened, and tell the user what to do or expect next.
use_when: After submitting a form, completing an application, or confirming a decision.
When the user has successfully completed their intended task.
do_not_use_when: The user still needs to provide more information in a later section.
The task has failed or encountered an error — use standard service error pages instead.

inputs.label: Reference number
inputs.description: Case, booking, or application reference for follow-up.

ux_considerations.text: Clearly state that the user's task has been successfully completed.
Explain next steps, expected timeframes, and whether the user needs to take further action.
Provide a clear way for users to get help or more information.
Do not display unnecessary personal or sensitive information.
Mask or partially hide identifiers where full details are not required.

technical_notes.text: Do not expose internal references or system details in confirmation messages.
Outcome status must be a structured, typed value — not a free-text message — to enable agentic processing.
